source_jobs.title: Director of Business Development
source_jobs.description:
DIRECTOR, BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT – GOVERNMENT & DEFENSE




ABOUT ANYSIGNAL

AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade. Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds spanning cloud computing, artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and more.




ROLE OVERVIEW

The Director, Business Development – Government & Defense will focus on the development and execution of our GTM and sales strategy across defense, national security, and government customers. This role is focused on driving growth within the Department of War (DoW), Intelligence Community (IC), civil space agencies, and defense prime contractors.



You will own the full lifecycle of strategic opportunities while helping position AnySignal as a critical communications infrastructure provider for mission-critical government and defense applications.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and maintain senior-level relationships across DoD, IC, civil agencies (e.g., NASA), and defense primes, including program executives, contracting officers, mission planners, and technical leadership (RF/payload/communications).

 - Lead end-to-end capture of government opportunities, including pipeline development, qualification, capture planning, teaming strategy, proposal development, negotiation, and post-award growth.

 - Identify, qualify, and pursue opportunities across key acquisition pathways (FAR-based contracts, OTAs, SBIR/STTR, IDIQs, and other transaction mechanisms).

 - Develop and execute account and campaign strategies aligned to priority agencies, programs of record, and emerging mission needs.

 - Establish and negotiate strategic agreements, including teaming agreements, CRADAs, pilot programs, and multi-year program engagements.

 - Partner closely with engineering and product teams to translate mission requirements into tailored technical solutions and proposals.

 - Lead and coordinate responses to RFIs, RFPs, BAAs, and other solicitations, driving cross-functional alignment across technical, pricing, contracts, and leadership stakeholders.

 - Maintain disciplined CRM practices, including pipeline tracking, forecasting, and reporting on bookings and growth metrics.

 - Provide voice-of-customer insights to shape product roadmap, positioning, and long-term strategy.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in a technical, engineering, or business-related discipline.

 - 5+ years of business development, capture management, or program-facing experience within the U.S. government, defense, or aerospace sectors.

 - Demonstrated success winning and managing complex government opportunities from early shaping through contract award.

 - Strong understanding of U.S. government acquisition processes, contracting vehicles, and budget cycles.

 - Experience working with or alongside defense primes, government agencies, or national security customers.

 - Proven ability to build and manage a qualified pipeline and consistently achieve growth targets.

 - Strong communication and executive engagement skills, including briefing senior government and industry stakeholders.

 - Ability to travel (Up to ~50%) within the United States.

 - Eligible to obtain or currently hold TS/SCI clearance




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with space-based systems, RF communications, SATCOM, or related mission domains.

 - Prior experience in high-growth startups or scaling organizations.

 - Active U.S. security clearance (or ability to obtain one).

 - Familiarity with DoD and IC mission sets, space architectures, and emerging defense technology trends




COMPENSATION:

Expected OTE: $250-350k + Equity and Benefits




EXPORT CONTROL REQUIREMENTS


IN ORDER TO COMPLY WITH THE SPACE TECHNOLOGY EXPORT RULES OF THE U.S. GOV

source_jobs.title: VP of Product and Technical Strategy
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY

AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade. Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds spanning cloud computing, artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and more.





THE ROLE

We're hiring a VP of Product & Technical Strategy to define and drive product direction across our RF, DSP, and satellite communications product lines. Reporting to the CEO, you'll sit at the intersection of customers, engineering, sales, and leadership, translating complex mission requirements into product strategy, roadmap priorities, and clear technical narratives for the market.

This is a high-leverage, foundational role. As our first dedicated product leader, you'll establish the product function from the ground up, shape how a fast-growing hardware company makes its most important decisions, and work directly with a deeply technical founding team. The role is hands-on to start, with the mandate and runway to build a product team as the company scales.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the product roadmap across current and next-generation product lines, including variant strategy, prioritization of customer requirements, lifecycle planning, and the evolution from customer-specific solutions toward scalable product platforms.

 - Drive technical product strategy with customers. Engage as the senior product voice in technical customer conversations, reasoning through RF, DSP, and satcom requirements to shape opportunities. Build the frameworks that make opportunity qualification and solutioning repeatable across the organization.

 - Lead product positioning and enablement. Translate deep technical capability into crisp market-facing narratives: product briefs, capability matrices, competitive differentiation, and customer-facing roadmap stories that equip sales and marketing to represent the portfolio with precision.

 - Build the product operating cadence. Establish the lightweight rhythms that keep engineering execution, customer timelines, and business priorities connected as we scale, from roadmap reviews to requirements intake and customer commitment alignment.

 - Partner closely with the CEO and engineering leadership on portfolio strategy, build-vs-customize decisions, and long-term product architecture.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Deep technical fluency in RF systems, DSP, satellite communications, Software Defined Radio, or adjacent aerospace and defense hardware, with the credibility to engage senior engineers and technical customers as a peer.

 - A track record of translating complex customer requirements into product strategy and roadmap decisions for hardware or deeply technical systems products.

 - Strong commercial judgment: fluency in sales cycles, proposals, customer commitments, and market positioning.

 - The ability to bring product discipline to a fast-moving, engineering-led culture, adding clarity and alignment without adding bureaucracy.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication, from technical collateral to executive strategy.

 - An entrepreneurial operating style: comfortable with ambiguity, hands-on by default, and energized by building a function from zero.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience serving defense, national security, or space customers.

 - Hands-on background with SDR platforms, modems, waveforms, phased arrays, or ground systems.

 - Familiarity with government contracting and proposal processes (SBIR, BAA, OTA).

 - Experience scaling a technical business from custom engineering toward repeatable product lines.




WHY ANYSIGNAL

You'll define the product function at a company with real revenue, flight-proven hardware, and some of the deepest RF and communications expertise in the industry, at exactly the moment the company is scaling its p

source_jobs.title: Accountant
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY

AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade. Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds spanning cloud computing, artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and more.




THE ROLE

The Accountant will be a key member of AnySignal’s Finance team and the day-to-day owner of our accounts payable and accounts receivable functions. This role combines high-volume transactional processing across invoices, payments, collections, and credit card activity with growing ownership of general ledger, reconciliation, and month-end close responsibilities.

This is a development role on the accounting track. The right person will treat AR/AP as the foundation, not the ceiling. Over time, this role will expand into broader accounting ownership, including close activities, financial reporting support, process improvement, and internal controls. It is an ideal opportunity for someone who wants to build the technical foundation, judgment, and process ownership needed to grow into Senior Accountant, Accounting Manager, and eventually Controller-level responsibilities.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Accounts Payable

 - Own day-to-day accounts payable activity, including invoice entry, payment preparation, data validation, and documentation.

 - Process and match invoices to purchase orders, resolving discrepancies as needed.

 - Manage payment schedules, including ACH, wire, and check payments.

 - Monitor AP aging and ensure accuracy of vendor payment terms.

 - Respond to vendor and internal stakeholder questions in a timely and professional manner.

 - Verify accuracy of data entry and general ledger coding to ensure transactions are recorded to the correct accounts and departments.



Accounts Receivable

 - Prepare, review, and distribute customer invoices.

 - Manage collections activity and follow up on outstanding balances.

 - Process and reconcile customer payments and resolve payment discrepancies.

 - Perform weekly AR reconciliations and support cleanup of historical transactions.

 - Review and maintain customer deposit accounts.

 - Prepare and distribute weekly aging analysis to internal stakeholders.



General Accounting & Month-End Close

 - Own assigned month-end close activities across AR and AP, including account reconciliations, accruals, prepaids, and supporting schedules.

 - Prepare and post journal entries with appropriate supporting documentation.

 - Assist in maintaining the integrity of the general ledger and chart of accounts.

 - Reconcile bank, credit card, and balance sheet accounts, investigating and clearing reconciling items.

 - Support the preparation of financial statements and variance analysis.

 - Provide commentary on key drivers behind period-over-period changes.

 - Assist with audit preparation, schedules, and requests from internal and external auditors.

 - Maintain accurate financial records and support compliance with company policies and GAAP.



Process Improvement & Controls

 - Identify opportunities to streamline AR/AP, close, and reconciliation processes.

 - Improve data quality, documentation, and internal controls as transaction volume grows.

 - Help document accounting procedures and contribute to a scalable finance and accounting infrastructure.

 - Support ad hoc analyses, reporting, and special projects as needed.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.

 - 1–3 years of accounting experience, ideally including accounts payable and/or accounts receivable.

 - Strong new graduates with relevant internships and a clear interest in the accounting track will also be considered.

 - Solid understanding of accounting fundamentals and GAAP.

 - Experience supporting month-end close is a

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer (Python)
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY

AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade. Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds spanning cloud computing, artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and more.




THE ROLE

As a Python Software Engineer, you will own AnySignal’s data aggregation, test, and automation framework that enables testing of hardware, software, and DSP systems. You will work closely with a multidisciplinary team to empower rapid, repeatable testing of space and ground segment components, and will develop a software framework that empower rapid iteration and triage of system functionality.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and maintain a Python software framework for engineers to write automated tests

 - Design and maintain systems for data aggregation and analysis

 - Build UIs that interface with hardware and software systems


REQUIRED SKILLS

 - Expertise developing Python libraries

 - Strong understanding of networking concepts

 - Ability to develop extensible and maintainable software systems that can smoothly support all new engineering projects across the company


DESIRED SKILLS

 - Expertise architecting scalable SQL and/or NoSQL databases

 - Experience developing test automation including equipment drivers, debug tools, and socket interfaces

 - Experience with Docker

 - Familiarity with RF and DSP concepts

 - Working knowledge of GNU Radio




EXPORT CONTROL REQUIREMENTS

To comply with U.S. Government space technology export regulations, including ITAR, applicants must be a U.S. citizen, lawful permanent resident, protected individual under 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or eligible to obtain the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.




EQUAL OPPORTUNITY & ACCOMMODATIONS

AnySignal is committed to building an inclusive and accessible workplace where all employees feel respected, supported, and able to contribute fully. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including women, veterans, individuals with disabilities, and members of underrepresented communities.

If you require accommodation at any stage of the hiring process, please contact us in confidence at careers@anysignal.com. We are committed to ensuring equal access for all applicants.

source_jobs.title: Digital Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade.



Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds that cover cloud computing to artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and much more.



As a Digital Electrical Engineer on the hardware team, you will be responsible for owning the design, manufacture, test and deployment of highly integrated PCBAs used by a large number of space customers. You will work with a small team of software, RF hardware, and FPGA engineers to shepherd the product from initial design to vehicle integration, taking a full-stack approach to building robust space communication systems.



Responsibilities

 - Design high-speed digital hardware including processors, FPGAs, memory, interfaces, and supporting circuitry

 - Perform trades and analyses of board architecture and component selection

 - Schematic capture and layout of space-grade PCBs

 - Bring-up and testing of new designs

 - Partner with RF engineers, software, mechanical, thermal, manufacturing teams and production test teams to drive designs end-to-end

 - Work with mechanical engineers to ensure proper thermal dissipation of devices

 - Work with supply chain counterparts to enable rapid manufacture and iteration of components

 - Design for performance under space-grade acceptance, qualification, and compliance requirements



Basic Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering, computer engineering, physics, or other STEM discipline

 - 3+ years of professional experience designing high-speed digital circuitry

 - Proven experience designing boards using EDA tools such as Altium Designer

 - Experience designing and debugging Xilinx RFSoC / MPSoC-based systems or equivalent platforms

 - Experience debugging SoCs across the bootup stack (baremetal/bootloader/linux)

 - Experience in modeling, simulation, and testing of digital and power electronics

 - Familiar with failure mode effects and criticality analysis (FMEA)



Preferred Experience & Skills

 - Experience designing and implementing mixed-signal circuit boards from concept through production using processors, FPGAs, Ethernet, multi-GHz Serdes, DRAM interfaces (DDR4), I2C, SPI, operational amplifiers, analog to digital and digital to analog converters, and power supply components

 - Deep understanding with configuration and interfacing of memory, volatile and nonvolatile

 - Experience designing power trees with complex sequencing requirements

 - Familiar with failure mode effects and criticality analysis (FMEA)

 - Experience selecting and monitoring components capable of performing in a radiation environment



In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), it is necessary to be a U.S. citizen, a lawful U.S. permanent resident, a protected individual as specified by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or possess eligibility to secure the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. For additional information on ITAR, please seek further details.



AnySignal values and fosters an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel respected, supported and able to participate. We welcome applications from: women, Aboriginal persons, persons with disabilities, ethnic minorities, visible minorities, veterans, people who identify as LGBTQIA2S+ and others who may contribute to diversification in our environment.



Should you have a need for accommodation, AnySignal will endeavor to meet your accommodation needs. At any stage of the recruitment and selection process you may make your needs for accommodation known through contacting our team, in confidence, by email to: careers@anysignal.com. We ai

source_jobs.title: Assembly and Test Technician
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY

AnySignal is developing the next generation of RF products and services for some of the most demanding space and defense missions. We’re building a flexible, high-performance platform that’s easy to deploy, monitor, and scale — enabling customers to bring advanced radio technologies to life faster than ever before.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a detail-oriented, hands-on Assembly & Test Technician to join our fast-growing production team. You will play a key role in assembling, testing, and preparing our hardware systems for deployment. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with mechanical and electronic components, is eager to learn, and thrives in a dynamic, high-precision environment. This role REQUIRES IPC certification that includes soldering techniques and extensive experience reworking PCBA’s. Certification to IPC-A-610, 7711, 7721 J-STD-001




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Assemble mechanical and electronic components using tools (including torque wrenches) following detailed procedures.

 - Apply staking materials for stability.

 - Use our production tracking software to track builds and workflow.

 - Execute basic test scripts and bash commands on hardware systems.

 - Record, document, and communicate test results clearly and accurately.

 - Collaborate with engineers to troubleshoot and resolve assembly or test issues.
   
   


REQUIRED SKILLS

 - High school diploma or GED with:

 - Some documented application of technical skills outside of the classroom (examples: extracurricular project teams, personal projects, internships, etc)

 - Associate's Degree, Trade School certification, or equivalent, or 3+ years of experience in a professional technical role that includes prototyping and development

 - Familiarity with basic electronics lab equipment such as DMMs, oscilloscopes, power supplies, microscopes, spectrum analyzers, etc

 - Certification to IPC-A-610, 7711, 7721 J-STD-001 (Space and Military Applications Electronic Hardware Addendum), and IPC CID/CID+

 - Ability to read and understand technical documentation, such as electrical schematics, datasheets, and mechanical drawings

 - Experience with quality control, inspection, and/or testing of products

 - Experience with IPC standards

 - Basic electrical understanding and understanding of fundamental electrical engineering concepts




BONUS (NOT REQUIRED)

 - Basic understanding of electronic debugging or electrical engineering concepts.

 - Prior experience in hardware production environments (aerospace, medical, RF, or similar).




WHY JOIN ANYSIGNAL?

 - Work on groundbreaking hardware systems for space and aerospace.

 - Build meaningful, hands-on skills with the support of a passionate, experienced team.

 - Receive competitive pay and opportunities for growth.

 - Be part of a fast-moving startup where your contributions matter.



In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), it is necessary to be a U.S. citizen, a lawful U.S. permanent resident, a protected individual as specified by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or possess eligibility to secure the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. For additional information on ITAR, please seek further details.



AnySignal values and fosters an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel respected, supported and able to participate. We welcome applications from: women, Aboriginal persons, persons with disabilities, ethnic minorities, visible minorities, veterans, people who identify as LGBTQIA2S+ and others who may contribute to diversification in our environment.



Should you have a need for accommodation, AnySignal will endeavor to meet your accommodation needs. At any stage of the recruitment and selection process you may make your needs for accommodation known through contacting our team, in confidence, by email to: careers@anysignal.com. We

source_jobs.title: People Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY

AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade. Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds spanning cloud computing, artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a People Operations Specialist to support the day-to-day execution of our People team. This role is critical to delivering a seamless employee and candidate experience while ensuring our core HR and talent operations run smoothly.

You’ll work closely with the Head of Talent & HR and company leadership to execute key People initiatives across onboarding, recruiting coordination, HR systems, and employee experience. This is a highly cross-functional role with strong exposure across the business and an opportunity to help build scalable processes as we grow.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Execute end-to-end onboarding from pre-boarding through new hire ramp

 - Ensure employees have the tools, access, and information needed for Day 1 success

 - Track onboarding progress and proactively resolve gaps

 - Continuously improve onboarding workflows and documentation

 - Manage scheduling, logistics, and interview materials

 - Maintain accurate employee records and ensure HR data integrity

 - Support HRIS administration (e.g., Trinet), including onboarding workflows and employee updates

 - Assist with performance review cycles, promotions, and compensation processes

 - Help ensure compliance with internal policies and applicable employment regulations

 - Support company-wide moments (e.g., all-hands, celebrations, milestone events)

 - Serve as a first point of contact for employee questions and requests




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1–3 years of experience in HR, recruiting coordination, operations, or a related field

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities and execute reliably

 - High ownership mindset with strong follow-through

 - Excellent communication skills and a service-oriented approach

 - Experience with HR systems (e.g., Trinet or similar) is a plus




PREFERRED EXPERIENCE

 - Experience in a high-growth startup or fast-paced environment

 - Exposure to onboarding programs, event coordination, or employee experience initiatives

 - Familiarity with HR processes such as performance reviews or compensation cycles

 - Experience partnering cross-functionally with teams like IT, Finance, or Recruiting




EXPORT CONTROL REQUIREMENTS

To comply with U.S. Government space technology export regulations, including ITAR, applicants must be a U.S. citizen, lawful permanent resident, protected individual under 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or eligible to obtain the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.




EQUAL OPPORTUNITY & ACCOMMODATIONS

AnySignal is committed to building an inclusive and accessible workplace where all employees feel respected, supported, and able to contribute fully. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including women, veterans, individuals with disabilities, and members of underrepresented communities.

If you require accommodation at any stage of the hiring process, please contact us in confidence at careers@anysignal.com. We are committed to ensuring equal access for all applicants.

source_jobs.title: Ground Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade.



Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds that cover cloud computing to artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and much more.



We are seeking a versatile Ground Segment Software Engineer to join our team. This role is central to our mission, focusing on building robust ground segment software for space communications. You will play a pivotal role in managing and developing GNURadio modules, orchestrating flowgraphs, and deploying cloud-based software solutions.



Responsibilities

- Manage and deploy containerized services for ground segment operations in the cloud and at the edge.

- Implement and maintain cloud-deployed software systems.

- Collaborate with a cross-functional team to integrate RF technologies with cloud platforms.

- Contribute to the instrumentation and optimization of satellite communication flowgraphs.

- Ensure reliability and efficiency of ground segment software in alignment with mission-critical objectives.



Preferred Qualifications

- Bachelor’s or higher degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.

- Proven experience in Python

- Proficient in developing and debugging software for Linux environments.

- Experience with cloud platforms and software deployment in cloud environments.

- Prior experience in satellite communication systems or aerospace sector is a plus.

- Knowledge of digital signal processing and embedded systems.



AnySignal values and fosters an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel respected, supported and able to participate. We welcome applications from: women, Aboriginal persons, persons with disabilities, ethnic minorities, visible minorities, veterans, people who identify as LGBTQIA2S+ and others who may contribute to diversification in our environment.



Should you have a need for accommodation, AnySignal will endeavor to meet your accommodation needs. At any stage of the recruitment and selection process you may make your needs for accommodation known through contacting our team, in confidence, by email to: careers@anysignal.com. We aim to provide equal access to all applicants with disabilities, please do not hesitate to contact us about accommodations.

source_jobs.title: Supply Chain Buyer
source_jobs.description:
AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade.

 

Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds that cover cloud computing to artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and much more.

 

Role

As a Buyer at AnySignal, you will be responsible for driving the success of our supply chain operations by owning day-to-day purchasing, inventory management, and vendor coordination. This is a highly hands-on role: you will manage purchasing cycles directly, build and maintain data models in Excel/Sheets, and collaborate closely with engineers and contract manufacturers to ensure materials are ready for every build.

 

You will play a critical role in enabling new product introduction (NPI) hardware builds, ensuring parts and assemblies arrive on time, and identifying cost efficiencies across the supply chain. Your work will directly influence the pace and efficiency of our hardware development and production.



Responsibilities

 - Source and purchase a wide range of electronic and mechanical components in alignment with BOM requirements.

 - Own the NPI purchasing process, ensuring boards and components are procured and delivered for engineering test builds.

 - Place, track, and manage purchase orders with contract manufacturers and suppliers; mitigate risks and recover schedule slips.

 - Maintain inventory accuracy in systems (NetSuite, ION, etc.), including safety stock levels, consumption tracking, and vendor coordination.

 - Perform receiving, inspection, and kitting of parts when required for critical builds.

 - Collect, create, and analyze supply chain data—including purchase price variances (PPVs)—to identify savings and drive efficiency.

 - Build and maintain spreadsheet models for BOMs, budgets, inventory tracking, and forecasting. Collaborate with engineering on DFM, BOM updates, and ECO integration to ensure seamless transitions from design to production.

 - Partner with production, logistics, and finance teams to align material readiness with build schedules.



Required Skills

 - 3+ years of purchasing and supply chain experience in hardware-focused industries (space communications, aerospace, PCBAs, robotics, or similar).

 - Proven ability to independently own and execute purchasing processes end-to-end

 - History of purchasing EEE components

 - Advanced Excel/Sheets skills (pivot tables, index-match, conditional formatting; VBA/scripts a plus).

 - Strong data analysis capabilities and experience working with ERP/MRP systems (NetSuite, SAP, ION, or equivalent).

 - Ability to interpret and manage multi-level BOMs and technical drawings.

 - Professional communication skills with both suppliers and cross-functional teams.

 - Strong problem-solving, organizational, and prioritization skills.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business, Engineering, or a related field.



Desired Skills

 - Experience in a startup or New Space environment where flexibility and speed are essential.

 - Familiarity with PCB assembly processes and electrical components.

 - Familiarity with IPC standards.

 - Exposure to qualifying parts to MIL-STD-883 / 810 or NASA 8739 standards.

 - Knowledge of environmental test flows (HTOL, vibration, thermal-vac).



AnySignal values and fosters an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel respected, supported and able to participate. We welcome applications from: women, Aboriginal persons, persons with disabilities, ethnic minorities, visible minorities, veterans, people who identify as LGBTQIA2S+ and others who may contribute to diversification in our environment.

 

Should you have a need for accommodation, AnySignal will endeavor to meet your accommodation needs. At any stage of the rec

source_jobs.title: Senior RF Systems Engineer - Ground Systems
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY

AnySignal is developing the next generation of RF products and services for some of the most demanding space and defense missions. We’re building a flexible, high-performance platform that’s easy to deploy, monitor, and scale — enabling customers to bring advanced radio technologies to life faster than ever before.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior RF Systems Engineer focused on Ground Systems, you will own the end-to-end RF performance of AnySignal's ground terminal hardware across frequency bands used for commercial, civil, and defense communications systems. This role involves end-to-end ownership as well as nitty-gritty design. You're the person who figures out why the link isn't closing, what's eating the noise figure, and how to squeeze every dB out of the system. You'll define field-deployable terminals, drive component selection and BOMs, integrate modem hardware, and build the test procedures that make our systems reliable no matter where they're deployed. You'll work across a tight-knit team of electrical, mechanical, software, and manufacturing engineers to deliver ground hardware that performs across a range of bands and mission profiles.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Define top-level RF system requirements for ground terminals, setting specifications for antenna interfaces, frequency conversion chains, RF front ends, and signal processing

 - Own end-to-end terminal RF performance — G/T, noise temperature, EIRP, link margin, and noise figure — through characterization, cascade modeling, and link budget analysis across LEO, MEO, GEO, and defense mission scenarios

 - Define, select, and manage RF front-end component BOMs — LNAs, SSPAs, feeds, diplexers, cables, connectors, and waveguide hardware — from specification through procurement

 - Troubleshoot RF system issues across the full receive and transmit chain, from antenna feed to modem, and drive integration and validation of modem hardware and waveform performance

 - Work with antenna and mechanical teams to ensure pointing accuracy, feed alignment, and aperture performance meet system requirements

 - Plan and execute RF system acceptance testing using VNAs, spectrum analyzers, noise figure analyzers, and power meters; write test procedures, characterization reports, and performance documentation

 - Support regulatory and compliance submissions including frequency coordination, band licensing, and emissions analysis


REQUIRED SKILLS

 - 5+ years designing and deploying RF systems or communication links, with direct hands-on experience

 - Strong command of system-level RF metrics - G/T, noise temperature, EIRP, noise figure, link budget, and fade margin - and proficiency with RF bench instrumentation (VNA, spectrum analyzer, noise figure analyzer, power meter)

 - Experience owning a component BOM — selecting and sourcing LNAs, SSPAs, filters, diplexers, and waveguide hardware from specification through procurement

 - Proficiency with MATLAB, Python, or similar tools for link budget modeling and RF system analysis

 - Field instinct — comfortable troubleshooting on a rooftop or remote site with limited resources


DESIRED SKILLS

 - Prior experience developing or commissioning ground terminals for space or defense programs

 - Experience with parabolic dish systems including feed installation, polarization alignment, and antenna control systems

 - Working knowledge of DVB-S2 or other satellite waveforms and modem-level integration

 - Experience with multi-band or frequency-agile systems including LO architecture, filtering, and band-specific propagation effects (rain fade, atmospheric absorption, scintillation)

 - Experience in RF lineup design at microwave frequencies — gain staging, compression, intermodulation, and harmonic management

 - Familiarity with ground system EMI/EMC, phased array or electronically steered apertures, and ITAR-controlled environments



In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such a

source_jobs.title: Embedded Controls Engineer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY

AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade. Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds spanning cloud computing, artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This role spans COTS integration and custom firmware development. You'll commission and tune drive hardware, close loops on real motors, and build embedded software for our own control electronics — all in service of high-accuracy tracking on deployed systems.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the motion control stack from controller design through field performance, including tuning, validation, and long-term reliability

 - Design, implement, and tune closed-loop control systems (cascaded PID, feedforward, gain scheduling) on real hardware

 - Implement trajectory generation and tracking algorithms for high-rate target profiles

 - Design and develop embedded firmware for a custom motor control board

 - Integrate with higher-level software systems for scheduling, telemetry, and remote operations

 - Instrument the control system for observability — logging, metrics, diagnostics — so we can monitor fleet health and debug remotely

 - Design calibration and self-check routines that keep performance tight over time and across deployed units

 - Build HIL/SIL test environments and validate control software before field deployment

 - Characterize system dynamics — friction, compliance, inertia — and translate into controller performance




REQUIRED

 - Real experience closing feedback control loops on real motors — not just simulation

 - C/C++ on embedded or real-time platforms (RTOS, bare-metal, or RT Linux)

 - Hands-on integration with servo drives, motor controllers, or similar electromechanical systems

 - Comfortable with hardware interfaces (SPI, CAN, RS-485, EtherCAT, GPIO)

 - Ability to read schematics and debug at the board level




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with commercial motion controllers (Galil, Delta Tau, PMAC, or similar)

 - Antenna pointing, telescope tracking, or robotics background

 - Firmware development for custom PCBs (MCU bring-up, BSP, peripheral drivers)

 - Python for tooling and test automation

 - Familiarity with satellite orbital mechanics or two-axis mount kinematics




EXPORT CONTROL REQUIREMENTS

To comply with U.S. Government space technology export regulations, including ITAR, applicants must be a U.S. citizen, lawful permanent resident, protected individual under 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or eligible to obtain the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.




EQUAL OPPORTUNITY & ACCOMMODATIONS

AnySignal is committed to building an inclusive and accessible workplace where all employees feel respected, supported, and able to contribute fully. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including women, veterans, individuals with disabilities, and members of underrepresented communities.



If you require accommodation at any stage of the hiring process, please contact us in confidence at careers@anysignal.com. We are committed to ensuring equal access for all applicants.

source_jobs.title: Senior RF Hardware Engineer
source_jobs.description:
AnySignal is building the future of performant, resilient and adaptive connectivity for the space economy.

As a Senior RF Hardware Engineer on the hardware team, you will be responsible for owning the design, manufacture, test, and deployment of highly integrated RF devices used by a surging wave of space customers. You will work with a small team of software, hardware, and FPGA engineers to shepherd the product from initial design to vehicle integration, taking a full-stack approach to building robust space communications systems.



Responsibilities

 - Develop and own RF products across PCB, box, and integration levels

 - Own trades and analyses on new designs and question requirements

 - Bring up, troubleshoot, and qualify new designs

 - Collaborate with Supply Chain and Manufacturing teams to enable large-scale manufacture of products



Required Skills

 - 5+ years of robust experience designing circuitry in RF bands across frontend and conversion chains

 - End-to-end PCB design experience, including schematic capture, layout, fabrication and assembly

 - Experience with electronic design and analysis tools such as Keysight ADS, Ansys HFSS, and Altium Designer

 - Excellent understanding of RF metrics such as S-parameters, P1dB, noise figure, channel power, and EVM

 - Excellent understanding of RF cascades, link budgets, and noise analysis

 - Experience with test instruments (oscilloscopes, power supplies, VNAs, spectrum analyzers)

 - Experience with low noise amplifiers, power amplifiers, mixers, ADCs/DACs, phased locked loops, oscillators, and filters



Desired Skills

 - Experience building complex PCBs in Altium Designer

 - Experience selecting components for radiation-susceptible environments

 - Experience working with AD9361, Xilinx RFSoC, and similar digital-analog conversion interfaces

 - Solid experience soldering and reworking boards (0402 SMP components, QFN packages)

 - Experience performing communications link modeling and solid understanding of DSP fundamentals

 - Knowledge of antenna design, simulation, and measurement in near-field/far-field regimes

 - Experience working with high-PAPR signals and knowledge of DPD concepts



Please feel free to apply even if you do not have 100% of the required skills listed

 

In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), it is necessary to be a U.S. citizen, a lawful U.S. permanent resident, a protected individual as specified by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or possess eligibility to secure the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. For additional information on ITAR, please seek further details.

 

AnySignal values and fosters an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel respected, supported and able to participate. We welcome applications from: women, Aboriginal persons, persons with disabilities, ethnic minorities, visible minorities, veterans, people who identify as LGBTQIA2S+ and others who may contribute to diversification in our environment.

We aim to provide equal access to all applicants with disabilities, please do not hesitate to contact us about accommodations.

source_jobs.title: EMC Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Role Summary



AnySignal is ushering in the next generation of RF products and services, for the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade.



Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds that cover cloud computing to artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and much more.



As an EMI/EMC engineer you will lead the development, analysis and test execution to ensure system performance and resilience against electromagnetic environmental effects (E3). You will perform exhaustive analysis, design, and tests at the board, subsystem, and system levels. You will help solve EMI compatibility issues, running root cause investigations and collaborating with electrical, DSP, software, mechanical, and manufacturing teams to improve the reliability, resilience, and performance of the products.



Responsibilities



 - Lead physics-based requirements definition, modeling and simulation, rather than based on industry standard compliance – a deep understanding of electromagnetic environmental effects is critical.

 - Develop and own E3 analysis, mitigation and test across products, spanning LEO to deep space environments.

 - Design and implement EMI/EMC test campaigns, including requirements flow-down interfacing with external test labs and facilities

 - Drive creation of internal tools, databases, and automated test frameworks for consistent capture and accessibility of effects and mitigations

 - Represent EMI/EMC engineering in design reviews, trade studies, and program milestones

 - Interface with, teach, and collaborate with EE, ME, DSP, software and test engineers to architect robust designs, test and validate systems



Basic Qualifications



 - Bachelor’s or advanced degree in Electrical Engineering, Physics, Aerospace Engineering, or a closely related technical discipline.

 - 5+ years of professional experience in electromagnetic environmental effects (E3), EMC/EMI engineering or related fields.

 - Experience with radio test equipment such as spectrum analyzers, vector signal analyzers, vector signal generators, and network analyzers.

 - Strong coding/scripting skills (e.g., Python, MATLAB)

 - Demonstrated experience defining and executing E3 test campaigns



Preferred Experience & Skills



 - Advanced degree in electrical engineering, physics, or other engineering discipline.

 - 7+ years of professional experience in electromagnetic environmental effects (E3), EMC/EMI engineering or related fields.

 - Experience with MIL-STD-461, MIL-STD-464, MIL-STD-1542, FCC radio and EMC standards, and requirements.

 - Demonstrated experience troubleshooting EMI/EMC related issues from component to system level.

 - Strong knowledge of E3 fundamentals, RF engineering, and electrical circuit analysis.



Please feel free to apply even if you do not have 100% of the required skills listed



In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), it is necessary to be a U.S. citizen, a lawful U.S. permanent resident, a protected individual as specified by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or possess eligibility to secure the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. For additional information on ITAR, please seek further details.



AnySignal values and fosters an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel respected, supported and able to participate. We welcome applications from: women, Aboriginal persons, persons with disabilities, ethnic minorities, visible minorities, veterans, people who identify as LGBTQIA2S+ and others who may contribute to diversification in our environment.

source_jobs.title: Head of Quality
source_jobs.description:
Company

AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade. Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds spanning cloud computing, artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and more.



Role Summary

We are seeking a Head of Quality to build and lead AnySignal’s quality function across development and production. This role combines hands-on ownership of inspection and quality execution with strategic leadership to scale our quality systems, team, and culture.

You will be responsible for ensuring product reliability and compliance from early design through manufacturing and test, while evolving our Quality Management System (QMS) into a scalable, high-impact system. This is a highly cross-functional role partnering closely with Engineering, Manufacturing, and Supply Chain to improve yield, prevent escapes, and enable mission success.




RESPONSIBILITIES


QUALITY LEADERSHIP & STRATEGY

 - Build, lead, and develop a team of Quality Engineers, Specialists, and Inspectors

 - Define and execute the company’s quality strategy across the full product lifecycle (design → manufacturing → integration & test)

 - Establish quality standards, metrics, and operating rhythms that scale with company growth

 - Drive a culture of quality grounded in first principles, simplicity, and continuous improvement


INSPECTION & MANUFACTURING QUALITY

 - Own incoming, in-process, and final inspection processes for parts and assemblies

 - Oversee inspection workflows, prioritization, and efficiency across production

 - Ensure inspection methods, tooling, and metrology systems are robust, calibrated, and scalable

 - Partner with Manufacturing and Engineering to improve yield and reduce rework


NONCONFORMANCE & CORRECTIVE ACTION

 - Lead MRB processes including disposition, containment, and documentation

 - Own NCR/CAPA/RCA workflows end-to-end, ensuring timely closure and effectiveness

 - Identify systemic root causes and drive corrective actions across teams and work centers


QUALITY SYSTEMS (QMS)

 - Own and evolve the company’s QMS (ISO 9001 / AS9100 aligned) across all operations

 - Ensure processes are compliant while remaining practical and scalable for a fast-moving hardware environment

 - Develop and maintain documentation, policies, and standard work instructions

 - Support internal, supplier, and external audits


PRODUCT & PROCESS QUALITY

 - Partner with Engineering to review designs, identify risks, and improve manufacturability and testability early

 - Oversee development of quality plans including key characteristics, inspection strategies, and validation approaches

 - Ensure alignment across design, manufacturing, and supply chain to prevent quality escapes


SUPPLIER & CROSS-FUNCTIONAL QUALITY

 - Partner with Supply Chain on supplier quality, including incoming inspection, supplier corrective actions, and qualification processes

 - Establish strong collaboration with Engineering and Manufacturing to drive continuous improvement

 - Lead quality-related communication and alignment across the organization


METRICS & CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT

 - Define and track key quality metrics (yield, escapes, nonconformances, reliability trends)

 - Drive data-informed improvements across production and development

 - Implement and scale continuous improvement methodologies (Lean, SPC, DOE, etc.)




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or related field

 - 5+ years of experience in Quality, Manufacturing Quality, or related roles in a hardware production environment

 - 3+ years of experience leading teams or owning major cross-functional quality initiatives

 - Strong experience with inspection, NCR/MRB, CAPA, and RCA workflows

 - Ability to interpret technical drawin

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Company

AnySignal is developing the next generation of RF products and services for some of the most demanding space and defense missions. We’re building a flexible, high-performance platform that’s easy to deploy, monitor, and scale — enabling customers to bring advanced radio technologies to life faster than ever before.

 

About the Role

As a Technical Recruiter at AnySignal, you’ll be a key partner in helping us scale a world-class team of engineers, operators, and innovators. You’ll own full-cycle recruiting for a range of technical and operational roles — from sourcing and engaging exceptional candidates to driving an efficient, thoughtful interview process.

 

You’ll work closely with hiring managers, technical leads, and the People team to deliver a seamless and high-quality candidate experience while ensuring we meet aggressive hiring goals in a fast-paced, high-growth environment. This is a hands-on recruiting role for someone who thrives on impact — balancing strategic partnership with day-to-day execution.

 

Key Responsibilities

 - Manage full-cycle recruiting for primarily technical roles, with emphasis on sourcing, pipeline management, and candidate experience.

 - Partner with hiring managers to define role requirements, create effective sourcing strategies, and maintain structured interview processes.

 - Source candidates through direct outreach, referrals, and creative channels; maintain strong engagement through the interview lifecycle.

 - Utilize the Ashby ATS to manage candidate flow, ensure data integrity, and provide timely updates to stakeholders.

 - Drive pipeline volume and throughput consistently while maintaining a high rate of success on interview pass through and offer acceptance.

 

What Success Looks Like

 - Consistent delivery of 8–12 hires per quarter across assigned roles.

 - Pipeline data accuracy and clear visibility into recruiting metrics.

 - Positive candidate experience reflected in feedback surveys.

 - Increased sourcing volume and diversification of candidate pools.



Qualifications

 - At least 4 years of full life cycle recruiting experience (preferably in high-growth tech, aerospace, or engineering-heavy environments).

 - Demonstrated success sourcing and closing technical or hard-to-fill roles.

 - Strong command of modern recruiting tools (LinkedIn Recruiter, Ashby, or equivalent ATS).

 - Previous experience recruiting in ITAR or other highly regulated industries

 - Proven ability to manage multiple requisitions in a fast-paced, scaling company.

 - Excellent communication, follow-up, and relationship-building skills.



Bonus

 - Experience recruiting in AnySignal’s core disciplines — RF engineering, embedded systems, software engineering, or aerospace hardware.

 

In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), it is necessary to be a U.S. citizen, a lawful U.S. permanent resident, a protected individual as specified by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or possess eligibility to secure the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. For additional information on ITAR, please seek further details.

We aim to provide equal access to all applicants with disabilities, please do not hesitate to contact

source_jobs.title: Embedded Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
AnySignal is a startup whose mission is to usher in the next generation of RF products and services. For the most demanding space and defense missions, AnySignal provides a flexible platform with world-class performance that is easy to deploy, monitor, purchase, and upgrade.

 

Our team has a diverse range of backgrounds that cover cloud computing to artificial intelligence, communications and sensing, embedded systems, and much more.

 

As an Embedded Software engineer on the Space team, your work will traverse the entirety of the embedded stack, involving everything from low-level RF integrated circuit driver development to high-speed interfaces with the FPGA fabric to reliable networking and radiation-resilient software architecture. You will own software components that enable effective operation of space-grade hardware, and will work shoulder-to-shoulder with hardware and FPGA engineers to develop RF solutions that provide communications and sensing capabilities across multiple orbits and vehicle classes.



Responsibilities

 - Gather and review software requirements for space-faring applications

 - Write, debug, and test reliable code running on Linux-based embedded systems

 - Operate and automate hardware-in-the-loop setups to perform effective system and regression testing

 - Collaborate with hardware and FPGA engineers to rapidly develop features and squash bugs

 - Support deployment of software to customer vehicles



Required Skills

 - Proficiency in C++

 - Experience developing embedded software on on baremetal, RTOS, or Linux platforms

 - Knowledge of CMake, Make, and equivalent build tooling

 - Using debugging tools in the embedded stack

 - Experience building exhaustive unit and functional tests in a rapidly evolving codebase



Desired Skills

 - Experience developing reliable software for spacecraft or life-critical applications

 - Automating workflows and tests in Python

 - Proficiency in Git

 - Experience configuring and modifying CI pipelines

 - Experience working with the buildroot stack

 - Experience working with FSBL, U-boot, and embedded Linux

 - Experience writing software that interacts with the FPGA fabric

 - Experience developing on Xilinx MPSoC-based devices

 - Understanding of fundamental communications systems concepts, such as modulation, coding, and framing



In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), it is necessary to be a U.S. citizen, a lawful U.S. permanent resident, a protected individual as specified by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or possess eligibility to secure the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. For additional information on ITAR, please seek further details.

 

AnySignal values and fosters an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel respected, supported and able to participate. source_jobs.title: Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
AnySignal is building the future of performant, resilient and adaptive connectivity for the space economy.

 

As an Electrical Engineer on the hardware team, you will be responsible for owning the design, manufacture, and test of power circuitry for highly integrated PCBAs used by a large number of space customers. You will work with a small team of software, RF hardware, and FPGA engineers to shepherd the product from initial design to vehicle integration, taking a full-stack approach to building robust space communication systems.

 

Responsibilities

 - Design, analyze and test DC-DC power converters for space

 - Work on electrical power system and sub-system level design trades and develop requirements

 - Define and perform electrical power system verification testing and analysis

 - Work with multidisciplinary teams of hardware, software and production engineers to create new power electronics hardware

 

Basic Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering, computer engineering, physics, or other STEM discipline

 - 3+ years of professional experience designing power circuitry

 - Proven experience doing schematic capture and layout in Altium Designer or similar

 - Experience in modeling, simulation, and testing of power electronics

 - Experience designing and debugging power converters

 

Preferred Experience & Skills

 - Master’s degree in electrical engineering or related field

 - Familiar with failure mode effects and criticality analysis (FMEA)

 - Experience designing power trees with complex sequencing requirements

 - Experience with hand-on impedance measurement and loop-gain measurement

 - Working experience in voltage and current mode control and compensation design

 - Strong grasp of DC-DC topologies, with ability to select optimal architectures and components

 - Familiarity with a wide range of topologies such as push-pull, buck, boost, buck-boost, forward, half-bridge, full-bridge, LLC resonant and phase-shifted full-bridge converters

 - Familiarity with spacecraft electrical power systems

 

In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), it is necessary to be a U.S. citizen, a lawful U.S. permanent resident, a protected individual as specified by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or possess eligibility to secure the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. For additional information on ITAR, please seek further details.

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY

AnySignal is developing the next generation of RF products and services for some of the most demanding space and defense missions. We’re building a flexible, high-performance platform that’s easy to deploy, monitor, and scale — enabling customers to bring advanced radio technologies to life faster than ever before.




THE ROLE

We are looking for a versatile Forward Deployed Engineer to join our team. This role is a hybrid of network, customer success, forward deployment, embedded systems, and software engineering. You will play a critical role in installing embedded devices at customer sites to facilitate advanced space missions, managing data flow through software-defined radios (SDRs), and ensuring robust performance of network systems.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Install and configure embedded devices at customer sites.

 - Provide technical support and guidance to customers during installation and deployment processes.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless integration of RF technologies with customer systems.

 - Develop and maintain networking protocols and software for embedded Linux, cloud, and lab environments.

 - Ensure the reliability and performance of network systems in terrestrial and space applications.

 - Implement and manage containerized services for network operations.

 - Provide feedback on architecture, design, and code to ensure software systems meet requirements.

 - Contribute to the instrumentation and optimization of communication software.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 4+ years of experience in developing software for Linux-based systems.

 - Expertise in C++, C, and/or Python.

 - Experience with GNURadio.

 - Strong knowledge of networking protocols (TCP, UDP, etc.).

 - Experience with software-defined radios (SDRs) and RF technologies.

 - Familiarity with cloud platforms and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes, etc.).

 - Proven ability to write production-quality code.

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to interact with customers and cross-functional teams effectively.

 - Knowledge of digital signal processing and embedded systems.

 - Formal education in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.




COMPENSATION

Expected Base Salary Range: $130-200k



In order to comply with the space technology export rules of the U.S. Government, such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), it is necessary to be a U.S. citizen, a lawful U.S. permanent resident, a protected individual as specified by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or possess eligibility to secure the necessary authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. For additional information on ITAR, please seek further details.

AnySignal values and fosters an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel respected, supported and able to participate. We welcome applications from: women, Aboriginal persons, persons with disabilities, ethnic minorities, visible minorities, veterans, people who identify as LGBTQIA2S+ and others who may contribute to diversification in our environment.

notes: Highly technical roles, diverse backgrounds, and a focus on RF products and services suggest an engineering-heavy primary function.
